title: practical english usage - Part 311
content:
(not simply a period of time).

And we  use during when we  are talking about an event, activity or experience not The shop's closed during all of August. (     .. . in all of August.) We   use during to stress that we  are talking about the whole of the period.

We'll be away during/in August. I woke up during/in the night.

period of time.

We   use both during and in to say that something happens inside a particular 441 during       and   in For for, since, in and from, ►    469.

I'll come and see you for a few minutes during the afternoon.

- It rained during the night for two or three hours.

My father was in Germany for six weeks. (     .. .during six weeks.) - My father was in Germany during the summer.

During is used to say when something happens; for is used to say how  long 440 during       and   for not His success was due to his mother. (     .. .was owing to-his mother.) Due to can also follow the verb be. Owing to is not usually used like this.

this way, but the structure is common in educated usage.

Some people believe it is incorrect to use clue to at the beginning of a clause in Word  Problems from A  to Z     Section 31 vocabulary •  443   economic and economical I think a wide black belt would look very effective with that dress.

My headache's much  better. Those tablets really are effective.

If something is effective, it has the right effect: it solves a problem or gets a result.

Our  bus service is even less efficient than our train service.

He's not very efficient: he works very slowly, and he keeps forgetting things.

without wasting time or energy.

If somebody/something is efficient, he/she/it works in a well-organised way 444 efficient         and   effective ►    410 educate        and   bring      up with the truth.' ‘I was not lying' said the Minister. 'I was just being economical an economical little car an economical housekeeper Economical means 'not wasting money, time or effort’ .

economic theory economic problems Economic refers to the science of economics, or to the economy of a country.

443 economic          and   economical Denmark is about 500km  to the east of Scotland.

I live in the east of Scotland.

Note the difference between in the east, etc of. .. and to the east, etc of...

The sun rises in the east. By sunrise we were driving south.

I live in north London. There's a strong north wind.

We spent the winter in southern California.

In other cases, adjectives, nouns and adverbs generally begin with small letters.

Unemployment is high in the North. My sister lives in the South-west.

defined regions.

And capitals are used in the West, the North, etc when these are names of well- North Carolina Western Australia when these come  in official or well-established place names.

Capital letters are used at the beginning of East, Eastern, North, Northern, etc 3 capital     letters a southern accent  a group of northern poets We   use eastern, northern, etc to mean 'belonging to’  or ‘ typical of.

2  'belonging       to' the North/South Atlantic/Pacific the Northern/Southern hemisphere but South Australia   Western Australia; the Northern Territory but East/South, etc Asia   Western/Eastern, etc Europe Northern Ireland North/East/West Africa North/South America However, place names do not always follow this rule. Note the following: vocabulary •  447  else You're wearing somebody else's coat.

Else has a possessive else's.

What else would you like? (     Whrf-woidd youdike else?) Note that else comes immediately after the word it modifies.

When all else fails, read the instructions.

In a formal style, else is sometimes used after all.

much  else about his life.

We know when Shakespeare was born and when he died, but we don't know Whatever else he may be, he's not a mathematician.

Where else did you go besides Madrid?

I'm sorry. I mistook you for somebody else.

Would you like anything else?

question words; whatever, whenever, etc; little, much.

somebody, someone, something, somewhere; anybody, everybody, nobody, etc; We   use else to mean 'other* after: 1  use 447else electrical component electrical engineering electrical appliances electrical equipment Electrical is used before more general words.
